area: MSC 94D10 / 94A60 / 06E30 (+ 37P05, 11B83): Boolean function analysis, Walsh–Fourier spectra, cryptographic nonlinearity, T-function (Klimov–Shamir/Anashin) theory
hole_justification: Searches run (web/arXiv/IACR/MathOverflow-targeted): "Collatz T-function Klimov Shamir single cycle 2-adic"; '"Walsh spectrum" Collatz OR "3x+1" Boolean function'; '"Collatz" "circuit complexity" OR "Boolean circuit" lower bound'; "Anashin 2-adic ergodic Collatz triangular function"; '"Collatz" "T-function" mod 2^n invertible cryptography'; "Shallit Wilson 3x+1 finite automata"; "Collatz parity pseudorandomness linear complexity correlation PRNG"; '"linear cryptanalysis" OR "correlation matrix" Collatz'; "mathoverflow Collatz boolean Fourier carry"; 'Anashin "3x+1" OR Collatz van der Put 1-Lipschitz'. FOUND NOTHING on: Walsh spectra of Collatz steps/iterates, correlation-matrix (linear-cryptanalysis) analysis of T^k, or application of Klimov–Shamir/Anashin single-cycle T-function classification to Collatz. FOUND at the fringe: Sterin–Woods 2020 (mid-orbit bit prediction in NC1 but not AC0, quasi-CA embedding of base conversion — the one real circuit-theoretic result, known to us); Shallit–Wilson 1992 (T^k as finite transducers); Laarhoven–de Weger 2013 (Collatz graph mod 2^n IS the binary De Bruijn graph, isomorphism = Bernstein–Lagarias conjugacy); Sopin 2023 (HAL) extends Anashin measure-preservation criteria to p^k-Lipschitz maps and names 2-adic Collatz as THE motivating example — one line, no spectral analysis; applied Collatz-PRNG/hash papers (MDPI Entropy 2018, IACR eprint 2025/1606, Collatz-Weyl arXiv 2312.17043) are pure engineering with NIST tests, no Walsh analysis of T itself. The crypto toolkit side is mature but never pointed at Collatz: Wallén FSE 2003 and Schulte-Geers 2013 give the exact carry/addition spectrum; Anashin/Khrennikov–Yurova give ANF-coordinate ergodicity criteria.
bridge: Identify Z/2^n with F_2^n by bit coordinates. The Collatz step is T = S ∘ A where A(x) = x or 3x+1 branch-selected by bit x_0, and S = right shift. A is triangular (bit i of 3x+1 = x_i ⊕ x_{i-1} ⊕ carry_i(x_0..x_{i-1}) ⊕ [i=0]): ALL nonlinearity of a Collatz step is the carry function of x + (x<<1) + 1. T itself is NOT a T-function (the shift breaks triangularity; 2-adically T is 2-Lipschitz — exactly Sopin's observation). What IS a T-function: the Terras/Lagarias parity-vector map Q_∞: Z_2 → Z_2 (x ↦ (parity of T^k x)_k), an ISOMETRY (invertible 1-Lipschitz map = invertible T-function in Klimov–Shamir's sense) conjugating T to the 2-shift; Terras 1976 bijectivity mod 2^k IS the statement that Q_∞ is an invertible T-function, and Laarhoven–de Weger showed the mod-2^n Collatz graph is the binary De Bruijn graph via this conjugacy. Correspondence table: Collatz step mod 2^n ↔ cipher round function; T^k ↔ k-round cipher; parity vector ↔ systematic/known bits; Terras's exact uniformity of first k parities ↔ perfect k-round diffusion of the systematic bits; bias of w·T^k(x) for a linear mask w ↔ linear-cryptanalysis correlation; k-step correlations ↔ products over trails in Daemen's correlation-matrix formalism, whose trail graph here is exactly the De Bruijn graph. CORRECTION to the prompt's framing, discovered in verification: (1) full single-cycle classification of T-functions is really Anashin's 2-adic ergodic theory (Klimov–Shamir proved criteria for specific families like x + (x²∨C)); (2) the mod-2^n truncated DYNAMICS of Collatz is completely understood and contains zero Collatz-specific obstruction (it is the full De Bruijn shift) — so the productive targets are (a) the one-step correlation matrix and its k-fold trail composition, and (b) the coordinate Boolean functions φ_i(x_0..x_{i-1}) of the conjugacy Q_∞/Φ, where all the arithmetic content actually lives.
strongest_adjacent_theorems: Wallén (FSE 2003, LNCS 2887): complete classification of linear approximations of the carry function; the correlation of ANY linear approximation of addition mod 2^n is 0 or ±2^{-t} with t an explicit combinatorial count of active carry stages, computable in O(log n); full distribution of correlation coefficients determined. Since 3x+1 = x + (x<<1) + 1, this yields the exact Walsh spectrum of every Collatz step for free.

Schulte-Geers (Cryptography and Communications 5, 2013, 'On CCZ-equivalence of addition mod 2^n'): addition mod 2^n is CCZ-equivalent to a QUADRATIC vectorial Boolean function, with fully explicit Walsh spectrum — every Collatz step is quadratic up to CCZ-equivalence.

Klimov–Shamir (CHES 2002, LNCS 2523, pp. 470–483): x ↦ x + (x² ∨ C) mod 2^n is a permutation with a single cycle of length 2^n iff C ≡ 5,7 (mod 8); model theorem for bit-slice invertibility/single-cycle analysis of T-functions.

Anashin (1994; arXiv:1111.3093 'T-functions revisited') and Khrennikov–Yurova (arXiv:1303.6472): a 1-Lipschitz f: Z_2→Z_2 is measure-preserving iff its i-th coordinate function has ANF x_i ⊕ φ_i(x_0..x_{i-1}); ergodic (single cycle mod 2^n for ALL n) iff explicit parity/weight conditions on the φ_i (equivalently on van der Put coefficients) hold — the complete classification of single-cycle T-functions.

Anashin (arXiv:1112.5089): f is the function of a FINITE automaton iff its van der Put coefficients satisfy an explicit eventual-linearity criterion — a bridge between 1-Lipschitz 2-adic analysis and our regular-certificate/DFA program.

Terras 1976 / Lagarias 1985 / Bernstein 1994 / Bernstein–Lagarias 1996: Q_∞ is an isometry of Z_2 conjugating T to the 2-shift; first k parities are EXACTLY uniform on Z/2^k; the conjugacy is nowhere differentiable (Müller). Laarhoven–de Weger (Indag. Math. 24, 2013; arXiv:1209.3495): Collatz graph mod 2^n ≅ binary De Bruijn graph B(2,n), iso given by the conjugacy.

Sterin–Woods (RP 2020; arXiv:2007.06979): predicting ~half the bits of T^i(x) for i = O(log x) is in NC1 but NOT in AC0 — combined with Linial–Mansour–Nisan, this already implies the Fourier mass of mid-orbit Collatz bit-functions is not concentrated on low degrees, i.e., an implicit (unquantified) Fourier-hardness statement exists in the literature.
the_push: Two prongs, both untouched. PRONG 1 (correlation decay = quantitative mod-2^n randomization): Define the one-step correlation matrix Λ_n(u,w) = 2^{-n} Σ_x (-1)^{u·x ⊕ w·T(x)} on n-bit windows. Because the only nonlinearity is one carry chain, Wallén/Schulte-Geers machinery gives closed-form entries (0 or ±2^{-t}, sparse, explicitly structured). k-step correlations are trail sums (Daemen composition), and the trail graph is exactly the Laarhoven–de Weger De Bruijn graph — so k-step biases are governed by a weighted transfer operator on B(2,n). Goal theorem: for output masks w outside the 'systematic' parity-vector subspace, |corr(u·x, w·T^k(x) mod 2^n)| ≤ 2^{-ck} with explicit c > 0. This is strictly finer than Terras: Terras gives EXACT uniformity of the first k parities given x mod 2^k but says nothing about joint biases of high-bit masks or about windows n ≠ k; a correlation-decay theorem quantifies how fast ALL linear statistics of the window randomize and thus how long an orbit can maintain atypical LINEAR structure mod 2^n. It would interact with our DFA result: a regular divergence certificate induces persistent linear correlations mod 2^n (a DFA's acceptance parity is a bounded-degree function of window bits), so provable correlation decay constrains certificate size for-all-n, not just ≤7 states; it also constrains hypothetical cycles, whose parity vectors are eigenvector-like fixed structures of the trail operator (combinable with Hercher-type cycle bounds). PRONG 2 (spectral analysis of the conjugacy): compute Walsh spectra of the coordinate Boolean functions φ_i of the Bernstein–Lagarias conjugacy Φ (explicit 2-adic series: Φ(Σ2^{a_i}) = -Σ 3^{-(i+1)}2^{a_i}) — nobody has done this; conjecture near-flat spectra (near-maximal nonlinearity) as i grows, which would be the Boolean-analysis quantification of the known qualitative wildness (nowhere differentiable, non-semilinear per Monks); ANY proven deviation from flatness is exploitable arithmetic structure. HONEST LIMIT: mod-2^n analysis is provably blind to global divergence (De Bruijn fullness: every window pattern is realized by some integer), so this cannot alone decide the conjecture; realistic outputs are new quantitative theorems with provable constants, tightened structured-divergence exclusions, and sharpened inputs to density arguments (Korec/Krasikov–Lagarias style benign-path counting).
first_move: Lemma 1 (one-step spectrum, Lean-formalizable): derive from Wallén's carry classification the closed form for Λ_n(u,w) = 2^{-n} Σ_x (-1)^{u·x ⊕ w·T(x)} — entries are 0 or ±2^{-t} with t = number of active carry positions determined by the mask pair; verify exhaustively for n ≤ 16 by direct enumeration. In parallel on the 32-core machine: compute w·T^k(x) truth tables for all x < 2^n (n ≤ 24, k ≤ 40) and take fast Walsh–Hadamard transforms (n·2^n per output mask; full matrix for n ≤ 20, sampled masks above) to measure the empirical decay rate c in max_{u; w non-systematic} |corr| ≈ 2^{-ck}. Then prove c > 0 via an operator-norm bound on the |Λ_n|-weighted De Bruijn transfer operator restricted to the complement of the systematic subspace (spectral radius of a weighted De Bruijn adjacency is computable). Cross-check k=1 against Lemma 1 and the systematic subspace against Terras exactness.
payoff_odds: Realistic best case (~20%): a genuinely new theorem — 'linear correlations of Collatz iterates mod 2^n decay as 2^{-ck} outside the parity subspace, with explicit c' — the first quantitative mod-2^n randomization rate with provable constants, sharpening Terras, giving a for-all-DFA-size obstruction complementing our ≤7-state exhaustion, and a new constraint on cycle parity vectors. Likely case (~70%): exact one-step Walsh spectrum (new but modest), clean empirical decay, partial operator-norm bound, plus first-ever spectral data on the Bernstein–Lagarias conjugacy — a publishable bridge paper connecting the crypto toolkit to Collatz. Failure mode (~10%): the transfer operator has norm 1 on a larger-than-systematic subspace (hidden linear structure — which would itself be interesting). Conjecture-level impact: <1%; mod-2^n methods provably cannot see divergence alone (De Bruijn fullness), so this is a structure-and-constants play, not a proof strategy.

prior_art.ref: Stérin & Woods 2020, 'The Collatz process embeds a base conversion algorithm', RP 2020 / arXiv:2007.06979
prior_art.what: Quasi-cellular-automaton form of Collatz in binary/ternary; predicting ~half the bits of T^i(x), i=O(log x), is in NC1 but not AC0. The only real circuit-complexity result; implicitly Fourier-theoretic via LMN but no spectra computed.

prior_art.ref: Laarhoven & de Weger 2013, 'The Collatz conjecture and De Bruijn graphs', Indag. Math. 24:971-983 / arXiv:1209.3495
prior_art.what: Collatz graph mod 2^n is isomorphic to the binary De Bruijn graph; the 2-adic limit isomorphism is exactly the Bernstein-Lagarias conjugacy. Fully settles the mod-2^n truncated dynamics (no Collatz-specific structure there) — redirects the T-function push to the conjugacy and correlation structure.

prior_art.ref: Shallit & Wilson 1992, 'The 3x+1 problem and finite automata', Bull. EATCS 46
prior_art.what: Each iterate T^k is computed by a finite-state transducer; studies automaton growth. Automata-theoretic ancestor of the k-bit-window view; no spectral analysis.

prior_art.ref: Klimov & Shamir 2002, 'A New Class of Invertible Mappings', CHES 2002, LNCS 2523
prior_art.what: T-function invertibility and single-cycle criteria (x + (x^2 ∨ C) single-cycle iff C ≡ 5,7 mod 8). Never applied to Collatz by them or anyone since.

prior_art.ref: Wallén 2003, 'Linear approximations of addition modulo 2^n', FSE 2003, LNCS 2887
prior_art.what: Complete classification of linear approximations of the carry function; O(log n) exact correlation computation; correlation distribution. The exact spectral toolkit for the ×3 carry — never pointed at Collatz.

prior_art.ref: Schulte-Geers 2013, 'On CCZ-equivalence of addition mod 2^n', Cryptogr. Commun. 5:75-87
prior_art.what: Addition mod 2^n is CCZ-equivalent to a quadratic vectorial Boolean map; explicit Walsh spectrum. Means each Collatz step is quadratic-up-to-CCZ. Not applied to Collatz.

prior_art.ref: Anashin arXiv:1111.3093 ('T-functions revisited'); Khrennikov & Yurova arXiv:1303.6472; Anashin arXiv:1112.5089 (automata finiteness via van der Put)
prior_art.what: The real classification of invertible/single-cycle T-functions: measure-preservation iff coordinate ANF is x_i ⊕ φ_i(lower bits); ergodicity criteria via van der Put coefficients; finite-automaton criterion. The school's ONLY Collatz contact is naming it as a 2-Lipschitz (not 1-Lipschitz) example.

prior_art.ref: Sopin 2023 (HAL hal-04098731), 'Criteria of measure-preserving for p^k-Lipschitz mappings'
prior_art.what: Extends Anashin-style measure-preservation criteria beyond 1-Lipschitz, explicitly citing the 2-adic Collatz extension as 'a striking example of 2-Lipschitz measure-preserving ergodic mapping'. One example line; no Walsh/spectral analysis. The single clearest fringe datum.

prior_art.ref: Terras 1976; Lagarias 1985; Bernstein 1994; Bernstein & Lagarias 1996 ('The 3x+1 conjugacy map'); Monks & Yazinski (autoconjugacy)
prior_art.what: Parity-vector bijectivity mod 2^k; Q_∞ isometry conjugating T to the 2-shift; explicit inverse conjugacy series; nowhere-differentiability. This IS the invertible T-function at the heart of the bridge — stated 25 years before Klimov-Shamir, never analyzed with Boolean-Fourier tools.

prior_art.ref: Cloney, Goles & Vichniac 1987; 'Two Cellular Automata for the 3x+1 Map' (arXiv:nlin/0502061)
prior_art.what: Quasi-cellular-automaton binary representations of Collatz dynamics; qualitative complexity observations, no spectra.

prior_art.ref: Applied fringe: 'A Novel Image Encryption Scheme Based on Collatz' (Entropy 2018); Collatz-Weyl generators (arXiv:2312.17043); Collatz Hash (IACR eprint 2025/1606)
prior_art.what: Engineering uses of Collatz as entropy source with NIST statistical tests and avalanche measurements; zero mathematical analysis of T's Walsh spectrum. Confirms the analysis direction is untouched.

prior_art.ref: Karras & de Weger 2026, 'Determinants of modular Collatz graphs and variants', arXiv:2601.15463
prior_art.what: Very recent: determinants of adjacency-type matrices of Collatz graphs mod m; number-theoretic graph invariants, adjacent to but distinct from correlation-matrix analysis.
